skill.name: chronicle-project-tracker
skill.description: Manage Chronicle project development using database-tracked milestones, next steps, and roadmap visualization. Works with MCP tools (fast, structured) or CLI commands (portable). Use when planning features, tracking progress, viewing roadmap, or linking sessions to milestones. Eliminates manual DEVELOPMENT_HISTORY.md updates.
skill.summary: Track Chronicle milestones, next steps, roadmaps, and linked development sessions.
skill.author: ChandlerHardy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples and Chronicle tool references. The only confirmed risk is low: the skill documents local Chronicle CLI commands that can modify project-tracking state.

low_findings.title: Local Chronicle CLI Commands Modify Project State
low_findings.description: The external command findings are true positives for documented CLI usage. The commands are Chronicle-specific milestone, roadmap, next-step, and session-linking commands, so the risk is limited to intended local project metadata changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit Chronicle CLI examples and several are described as user-run actions. No shell metacharacter injection, remote download, or arbitrary command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts do not identify cryptographic code. The flagged lines contain prose, Markdown examples, field names, or command arguments such as descriptions and roadmap text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review found no MD5, SHA1, cipher configuration, password hashing, or cryptographic API usage. The static matches are token-level false positives in Markdown content.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Project Tracking Queries
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings are false positives. The referenced text queries Chronicle roadmaps, sessions, and milestone records, not operating system details or network targets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms appear in normal project-management workflows. No evidence of port scanning, host discovery, account enumeration, or external network probing was found.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is Plain Project Planning Text
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert at line 23 is dismissed. The line only lists development progress tracking as a use case.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line contains no network command, URL, socket operation, or scan target. It is a plain-language bullet in the skill usage section.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash command examples for the Chronicle CLI. These are legitimate for the skill purpose, but they can create, update, complete, or link local project tracking records.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed as command execution guidance, but the commands are scoped to Chronicle project tracking. There is no evidence of malware, exfiltration, or unsafe shell composition.

content.value_statement: Project history can become stale when milestones and sessions are tracked by hand. This skill uses Chronicle tools and CLI commands to keep roadmap, next-step, and progress data structured.
actual_capabilities: Lists Chronicle milestones by status, type, and priority.
Shows roadmap data for in-progress, planned, and completed work.
Creates milestone and next-step command examples for users to run.
Links development sessions to active milestones through Chronicle commands.
Generates progress report outlines from milestone and session data.
Explains Chronicle project tables used for milestones and next steps.
limitations: Requires Chronicle MCP tools or the Chronicle CLI to be installed.
Does not replace project management systems outside Chronicle.
May modify local Chronicle project metadata when CLI commands are run.
Report quality depends on complete milestone, session, and commit data.

use_cases.title: Plan Feature Milestones
use_cases.description: A developer can check existing roadmap items, define a feature milestone, and break it into next steps.

use_cases.title: Review Weekly Progress
use_cases.description: A technical lead can summarize completed milestones, active work, pending steps, and linked development sessions.

use_cases.title: Maintain Development History
use_cases.description: A documentation owner can generate history sections from completed milestones instead of writing updates manually.

prompt_templates.title: Show Current Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for current project status.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the Chronicle roadmap and summarize what is in progress, planned, and recently completed.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Feature Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a new feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the Chronicle roadmap for duplicates, then draft a milestone and three next steps for this feature: [feature description].

prompt_templates.title: Link Session Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow after a development session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest Chronicle session, identify the active milestone, and show the command to link them.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Progress Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced reporting from milestone and session data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a weekly progress report from Chronicle milestones, linked sessions, commits, durations, and pending next steps.

output_examples.input: What should I work on next?
output: A concise roadmap view with active milestones first.
High-priority planned milestones ranked by priority.
Pending next steps with estimated effort when available.

output_examples.input: Create tracking for a new API feature.
output: A milestone title and description aligned with the requested feature.
Several ordered next steps for design, implementation, tests, and documentation.
Chronicle CLI commands that the user can review before running.

output_examples.input: What did I accomplish this week?
output: A weekly summary of completed milestones and active work.
Linked session and commit context when Chronicle data is available.
A short list of remaining next steps and priorities.

best_practices: Review generated CLI commands before running them against Chronicle data.
Link sessions to milestones soon after work finishes.
Use clear tags and priority levels so roadmap queries stay useful.
anti_patterns: Do not create milestones without checking the roadmap for duplicates.
Do not mark milestones complete while next steps remain unfinished.
Do not rely on reports when session or commit links are missing.

faq.question: Does this skill require Chronicle MCP tools?
faq.answer: No. It can use MCP tools when available, or Chronicle CLI commands for portable workflows.

faq.question: Can it create milestones automatically?
faq.answer: It provides Chronicle commands and tool guidance. Users should review commands before applying changes.

faq.question: What project data does it use?
faq.answer: It uses milestones, next steps, linked sessions, related commits, tags, priorities, and completion dates.

faq.question: Is it suitable for non-Chronicle projects?
faq.answer: Only if the project uses Chronicle data structures or the Chronicle CLI for tracking.

faq.question: Can it generate progress reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize recent completed milestones, active work, pending steps, and linked session context.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The skill includes local CLI commands that can change Chronicle project metadata when a user runs them.
